title: omp rules.json
description: Machine-readable rule set for a project folder. Written by omp-init/codify/learn, read by omp-audit. The specificity field tracks the generic->specialized evolution (0 = pure preset, 1 = fully project-specific).

omp_version.description: omp schema/harness version this file was written against, e.g. '0.1.0'.

preset_origin.description: Which references/presets/*.md seeded this project, e.g. 'python-ml', 'johnny-decimal', 'generic'.

specificity.description: 0 = rules are pure preset (just deployed). 1 = fully project-specific (learned). Rises as omp-learn promotes observations into project-owned rules.

structure.description: Folder layout rules: which directories exist and what role each plays.

convention.description: Named layout convention if any, e.g. 'johnny-decimal', 'src-layout', 'kedro', 'flat'.

origin.description: Provenance for the specificity computation (see learning-protocol.md §4): 'preset' weight 0, 'inductive'/'learned' weight 1.

enforced.description: If true, omp-audit flags files that violate this dir's role.

naming.description: File/dir naming rules. Patterns are regex (Python re syntax).

applies_to.description: Glob the rule applies to, e.g. 'data/processed/*.parquet'. Scope it to the layer the rule targets (e.g. '*/' = root-level dirs only) so audit does not apply a regex to every basename in the tree.

regex.description: Optional Python regex the basename must match. Omit when the rule is purely a path_constraint (where a file type must live), not a basename-shape rule.

path_constraint.description: Optional location rule: files matching applies_to must (or must not) live under a directory. Expresses 'all .pkl belong under data/processed/' — which a basename regex cannot. audit flags files of this kind found outside must_be_under.

must_be_under.description: Relative dir the matched files must live under, e.g. 'data/processed'.

must_not_be_under.description: Relative dir the matched files must NOT live under.

origin.description: Provenance for the specificity computation: 'preset' (weight 0, shipped generic), 'inductive' (weight 1, observed at init scan), 'learned' (weight 1, promoted via omp-learn gate). See learning-protocol.md §4.

learned_refs.description: IDs of learned.md observations that were promoted into the rules above (provenance).
